subject: [RFC PATCH 37/45] mm/slub: kvmalloc — add __GFP_NORETRY to large-kmalloc attempt

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of kvmalloc's large memory allocation attempts by adding a __GFP_NORETRY flag to signal that the caller has a fallback path, such as vmalloc. This change prevents the page allocator from tainting previously clean superpageblocks in an attempt to satisfy the kmalloc request, which can lead to performance issues and wasted resources. The patch addresses a problem where large kvmalloc requests were inadvertently tainting clean superpageblocks, causing subsequent hugepage allocations to fail. The approach taken is to add the __GFP_NORETRY flag in the same conditional that strips __GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM, allowing the page allocator to skip unnecessary retries and return NULL immediately when it encounters this flag.
